Records.description: Three-loop banana in $D=2$ with exactly two equal internal masses ($m_2=m_3$), while $m_1$ and $m_4$ remain distinct. The period vector is five-dimensional $(\Pi_2, \Pi_{14}, \Pi_{12}, \Pi_{11}, \Pi_0)$. After a $GL(5,\mathbb{Z})$ change of basis the transcendental lattice becomes $T = H \oplus H \oplus \langle -6 \rangle$. Via the exceptional isomorphism $O(2,3) \sim Sp(4,\mathbb{R})$, the mirror map is a classical Siegel modular function on the Siegel upper half-space $H_2$ and the holomorphic period is a Siegel modular form of weight 1. The holomorphic period is realised as the determinant of the A-cycle period matrix of a family of genus-two hyperelliptic curves.
Records.familyName: three-loop banana (two equal masses, Siegel modular) / three-loop banana with two equal masses (Siegel modular periods)
Records.method: K3 periods, transcendental lattice analysis, GL(5,Z) basis change, Siegel modular forms, genus-two hyperelliptic period matrix, mirror map
Records.location: section 'Automorphic properties of three-loop banana integrals', subsection 'The two-equal-mass case m2=m3' (bananas_2.tex)

Records.description: Three-loop banana (sunrise) diagram with 4 internal propagators carrying 3 distinct mass scales $(m_1, m_1, m_2, m_3)$ between 2 vertices, forming a 2-point function. Previously existing pySecDec example used for benchmarking Disteval vs IntLib integrators.

Names: three-loop banana (two equal masses, Siegel modular)
three-loop banana with two equal masses (Siegel modular periods)
